assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response mentions surge settings and parallel upgrades but does not specify GKE's 20-node upgrade parallelism limit or explain that maxSurge doesn't override this constraint

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: The response acknowledges hitting upgrade capacity limits but provides no mathematical calculation showing 150 batches or time estimates based on the 20-node limit

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response clearly recommends staggered node pool upgrades across different weeks and mentions extending maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The response explicitly shows upgrading CPU pools in Week 1 and GPU pools in Week 2, demonstrating separation and prioritization

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: While the response mentions GPU pools being more expensive/scarce and suggests conservative surge settings, it doesn't specifically address scheduling around training/inference workload gaps

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response shows extending the maintenance window from 8 to 12 hours and demonstrates multiple window usage in the cluster splitting strategy

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: The response does not mention cluster autoscaler scaling limitations or how this affects upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: The response does not mention GKE node limits, either the 15,000 standard limit or the 65,000 AI workload limit in newer versions
